endpoints.name: GET /translate/braille
endpoints.description: Translate from English to Braille. This is what you use if you have a braille display. This API translates the English text into characters that a braille display understands and you can feed the translated text directly to the display.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{FUNTRANSLATIONS_API_KEY}} with your FUNTRANSLATIONS API KEY.

endpoints.name: GET /translate/braille/dots
endpoints.description: Use this to see which dots are enabled for each Braille letters. This is highly educational (to see which dots are enabled) and can potentially drive a non braille display which works on individual dots.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{FUNTRANSLATIONS_API_KEY}} with your FUNTRANSLATIONS API KEY.

endpoints.name: GET /translate/braille/html
endpoints.description: Translate from English to Braille Image characters. This is probably what you want to use if you are displaying braille in a browser.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{FUNTRANSLATIONS_API_KEY}} with your FUNTRANSLATIONS API KEY.

endpoints.name: GET /translate/braille/image
endpoints.description: Translate from English to Braille image characters. This is probably what you want to use if you are displaying braille in a browser.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{FUNTRANSLATIONS_API_KEY}} with your FUNTRANSLATIONS API KEY.

endpoints.name: GET /translate/braille/unicode
endpoints.description: Translate from English to Braille Unicode characters.
endpoints.notes: Replace {{FUNTRANSLATIONS_API_KEY}} with your FUNTRANSLATIONS API KEY.
